error_signature: the item has no binary field ‘data’ [item 0]
title: Missing binary data in Gmail node
symptom: When attempting to send an email with an attachment from Gmail, n8n throws an error stating 'the item has no binary field ‘data’ [item 0]'. This indicates that the expected binary data for the attachment is not present.
applies_when: Using Gmail node to send an email
not_this_if: The email does not have any attachments
The workflow is not triggered by a Gmail event
root_cause: The error occurs because the Gmail node is not receiving the binary data for the attachment correctly. This can happen if the attachment is not properly linked or if the workflow does not correctly handle the binary data from the Gmail trigger node.
fix_steps: Add a Merge Node after the Gmail trigger to ensure the binary data is correctly passed along.
Configure the Merge Node to combine the binary data with the email content.
Ensure that the Gmail node is set to use the correct property for the attachment.
agent_instruction: Insert a Merge Node after the Gmail trigger to handle the binary data for attachments.
